Avatars 45 offline generators · deterministic · ← icon gallery · docs

Type any seed — every style re-renders live, in your browser, offline. Same output as node scripts/avatar.mjs <style>:<seed>.
🎬 Native animation: none of these generators animate — the whole ecosystem outputs static SVG/PNG. To animate one, wrap it in CSS/Motion yourself — see the mini demo and animation.md.

Loading generators…

Reference-only — not wired into the offline CLI

HTTP APIs, other languages, browser-only runtimes, and the animation forks. Documented for completeness.

Avataaars (original)

needs-react · MIT / free commercial · source ↗
The original Pablo Stanley cartoon library. Offline SVG here is covered by DiceBear `avataaars` / `avataaars-neutral` — use those unless you need the original React options.
Reach it via: React: `avataaars` (React 17). Vanilla SVG: `Avataaars.create(opts)` via HB0N0/AvataaarsJs (GitHub-only).

Avataaars — animated forks 🎬

needs-react · MIT · source ↗
THE only avatar generators found with native animation. Both are lightly-maintained React forks of Avataaars, not canonical packages. See docs/avatars/animation.md.
Reach it via: `@gschoppe/avataaars` (opt-in idle CSS `@keyframes` — import dist/animations.css) · `@vierweb/avataaars` (React state expression-cycling + on-hover sequences).

Playful Avatars

browser-only · MIT · source ↗
A framework-free rewrite of Boring Avatars; identical SVG output, no React. For offline generation here use the `boring-*` styles.
Reach it via: Web component: `<playful-avatar name="x">`. Needs a DOM (customElements) — browser, not Node.

pixel-avatar-lib

browser-only · Apache-2.0 · source ↗
Canvas-based, so it renders in a browser, not to an offline SVG string. For 8-bit avatars here use `pixel-art` or `dither`.
Reach it via: React + `<canvas>`; a "DNA" string encodes hair/face/clothing/accessories.

Squareicon

needs-canvas · MIT · source ↗
Dropped from the offline toolchain to keep it pure-JS (no native build). `identicon-js` and `blockies` cover GitHub-style / blocky identicons.
Reach it via: Node, but depends on the native `canvas` module and outputs PNG.

Robohash

http-api · Free (attribution appreciated) · source ↗
Hosted only — no offline lib. For offline robots use DiceBear `bottts`. The `.gif` output is a single static frame, not animation.
Reach it via: HTTP: `https://robohash.org/<text>?set=set1..set5&bgset=bg1|bg2&size=200x200`. Sets: robots / monsters / robot-heads / kittens / human heads.

UI Avatars

http-api · Free · source ↗
Hosted initials avatars. Offline equivalent here: DiceBear `initials`.
Reach it via: HTTP: `https://ui-avatars.com/api/?name=Erick&background=random&format=svg`.

python_avatars

other-language · MIT · source ↗
Python, not JS — use in Python projects. Cartoon SVG in the Avataaars lineage.
Reach it via: PyPI `python-avatars` (Python). Pure-SVG, add your own clothes/hair/eyes.

pixelated-avatar-generator

other-language · MIT · source ↗
Haskell, not JS. For offline pixel PNGs here use `blockies`.
Reach it via: Haskell library/CLI; PNG output from a 32-char hex (MD5) seed.

Personas by Draftbit

browser-only · MIT (repo) · source ↗
Not on npm. The style ships offline here as DiceBear `personas`.
Reach it via: Unpublished React + ReScript + html2canvas app (repo is `private`).
